Inferred responsibility: Return hyperlink display target or click target.

Evidence: Deletes cached font object.

Inferred responsibility: Application entry/main message loop.
Evidence: Registers main window plus `DiagOutWindow` and `DiagInWindow`, loads RichEdit DLL, creates main window, loads accelerators, runs `GetMessageA`/`TranslateMessage`/`DispatchMessageA`.

Inferred responsibility: Main window procedure and central command dispatcher.
Evidence: Registered as main class wndproc; handles many `WM_COMMAND` IDs, opens dialogs, launches map/minimize/import/export/save flows, calls `DefWindowProcA` fallback.

Inferred responsibility: MINTIME dialog procedure for configuring and returning minimize-time benchmark parameters. It initializes numeric spinner controls, radio/check boxes, enables/disables percent-true input, and on OK writes selected inputs/outputs/repetitions/percent mode/exact-fast/log flags back to the caller parameter block.
Evidence: Passed to DialogBoxParamA(..., "MINTIME", ..., FUN_00409747, &local_13f0) in FUN_004016d2. Handles WM_INITDIALOG and WM_COMMAND, initializes up-down controls 0x484/0x486/0x488/0x48a, reads edit controls 0x483/0x485/0x487/0x489, checks controls 0x408/0x42a/0x48b/0x48c, and caller uses the returned fields for MINIMIZE TIME TEST timing runs with FUN_0040c55b.

Inferred responsibility: Recreates or imports a stored gate diagram into the active diagram-entry UI by replaying gate/object placement and wire-connection actions.
Evidence: Starts diagram entry with SendMessageA(DAT_00452aac, WM_COMMAND, 0xcd, 0), iterates saved diagram objects at param_1 + 0x3a4 / count param_1 + 0x1650, maps saved object types to gate-palette command IDs 0x3f4..0x402, 0x430, 0x438, 0x439, sends mouse/command messages to DAT_00452a98, uses FUN_0040a1fb for Variable Name dialogs, then replays wire connections using stored pin coordinates and source indexes.

Inferred responsibility: Worker thread that auto-fills and accepts the modal Variable Name dialog during scripted diagram reconstruction/input-output node insertion.
Evidence: FUN_00409b22 sends command 0x438/0x439, then starts FUN_0040a1fb with __beginthread; this function waits for FindWindowA("#32770", "Variable Name"), writes dialog item 0x43e via SetDlgItemTextA, sends WM_COMMAND IDOK, then exits via FUN_0043ea5f. For command 0x439 it forces name "X"; otherwise it uses the supplied name pointer.

Inferred responsibility: Central UI error-message dispatcher: decodes a packed Logic Friday error code, formats the matching message, and shows it with MessageBoxA.
Evidence: Switches on param_2 >> 0x10, formats strings such as "Syntax error in equation.", "No function is selected.", "Unable to map the function.", and "File error %d.", then calls MessageBoxA(param_1, local_10c, caption, 0). Call sites pass packed constants like 0x40001, 0x50000, 0x1a0000, 0x2d0040, and 0x2f000a from startup, selection validation, minimization/mapping, parsing, file, and external-tool flows.

Inferred responsibility: Thin Win32 dialog-procedure wrapper for the TTSETUP truth-table setup dialog; initializes/caches the dialog context on WM_INITDIALOG, then delegates all message handling to FUN_00418362.
Evidence: Called as DialogBoxParamA(..., "TTSETUP", ..., FUN_0040ad29, 0x46bf70). Before the call, 0x46bf70 is populated with &DAT_0046c2d0 and &DAT_00452b30. On WM_INITDIALOG, FUN_0040ad29 stores lParam globally, then delegates to FUN_00418362, which initializes input/output count controls, validates variable names, writes labels/counts into the truth-table data block, allocates output truth-table arrays sized 1 << inputCount, and ends the dialog.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure thunk for the gate-mapping options dialog MAPDIALOG; binds the current function/model object, then forwards dialog messages to the object-specific handler that builds user.genlib from selected gate types.
Evidence: Main gate mapping command calls FUN_0040ad72(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1ac), then DialogBoxParamA(..., "MAPDIALOG", ..., FUN_0040ad72, 0). Message 0x8008 stores param_4 in DAT_0046c4d8; later messages call FUN_00422090(DAT_0046c4d8, hwnd, msg, command). FUN_00422090 handles WM_INITDIALOG/WM_COMMAND, checks NAND/NOR controls, validates required gate type selection, writes GATE nand*/nor* definitions to %s\\user.genlib, and ends the dialog.

Inferred responsibility: VARNAMEDLG dialog-procedure trampoline for adding a new gate-diagram input/output variable name.
Evidence: Called as DialogBoxParamA(..., "VARNAMEDLG", ..., FUN_0040adb4, gateType) when placing gate types 0x438 / 0x439. It saves param_4 into DAT_0046c4dc on message 0x8008; otherwise calls FUN_00435e82. The delegate initializes edit control 0x43e, validates the entered name with FUN_00436863, reports duplicate/invalid-name errors, appends accepted names to the input-name array for 0x438 or output-name array for 0x439, and closes the dialog.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure thunk for the Variable Name dialog used to rename an existing input/output variable node in the diagram editor.
Evidence: Handles custom message 0x8008 by storing the current function/model pointer in DAT_0046c4e0, otherwise delegates to FUN_004362c1. DialogBoxParamA(..., "VARNAMEDLG", ..., FUN_0040adf6, selectedIndex) is invoked on double-click of diagram objects whose type is 8 or 9. The delegate initializes edit control 0x43e, validates via FUN_00436863, updates input/output name arrays and the selected diagram object label, then calls EndDialog.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure trampoline for the gate-label edit dialog GATELABELDLG; stores the active diagram/controller pointer, then delegates dialog handling to FUN_00436748, which edits the selected non-input/output gate object's label text.
Evidence: Main diagram-entry setup calls FUN_0040ae38(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1a4), and the function stores param_4 in DAT_0046c4e4. On double-click in the diagram editor, non input/output objects use DialogBoxParamA(..., "GATELABELDLG", ..., FUN_0040ae38, selectedIndex), while type 8/9 variable nodes use VARNAMEDLG. FUN_00436748 handles WM_INITDIALOG, saves selected object index, limits edit control 0x43e to 0x10 chars, loads current label from selected object offset 0x50, and writes GetDlgItemTextA(..., 0x43e, ..., 0x11) back before EndDialog(1).

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure trampoline for the modeless Trace Gate Logic dialog TRACEDLG16. It binds the active gate-diagram/function object, then delegates dialog behavior to FUN_00436c32, which displays/toggles input values, recomputes traced gate states, and redraws the diagram with trace coloring.
Evidence: Call sites invoke FUN_0040ae7a(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1ac), then CreateDialogParamA(..., "TRACEDLG16", ..., FUN_0040ae7a, 0). Menu command 0x152 corresponds to Trace Gate Logic; toggling it creates/destroys this dialog and sends diagram commands 0x8013/0x8014. FUN_0040ae7a stores the object pointer in DAT_0046c4e8 on message 0x8008, otherwise calls FUN_00436c32. The delegate initializes up to 16 input controls 0x45d..0x46c, labels them with "%s = %d", toggles truth values, recomputes/redraws, invalidates the diagram window, handles Tracing_logic.htm help, and closes through command 0x152.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog-procedure trampoline for the IC Package Info dialog; stores the selected function/model pointer, then delegates dialog initialization/close handling to the model-specific handler that displays precomputed IC package counts.
Evidence: Main command 0x156 opens DialogBoxParamA(..., "ICPKGINFO", ..., FUN_0040aebc, 0) after exactly one function is selected. FUN_0040aebc(NULL, 0x8008, 0, DAT_0046c1ac) stores the active model pointer in DAT_0046c4ec; normal messages delegate to FUN_0042424d, which handles WM_INITDIALOG by setting dialog item 0x43e from this + 0x274 and closes on OK/Cancel. FUN_00423a22 fills that buffer with lines such as IC\\tQty, Hex Inverter, Quad 2-Input NAND, Quad 2-Input MUX, and TOTAL PACKAGES from mapped diagram gate counts.

Inferred responsibility: Print dialog hook/trampoline for Logic Friday's custom PRINTDLG template; forwards print-dialog messages to the global print-controller object so the dialog can choose between diagram versus equation printing and open page setup.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_0041c581(&DAT_00453ce8, hwnd, msg, commandId). FUN_0041a687 installs it into PRINTDLGA as the print hook, sets template name "PRINTDLG", title "Logic Friday: Printing", then calls PrintDlgA. FUN_0041c581 initializes radio buttons 0x425/0x426, disables diagram printing when the selected function has no diagram objects, updates mode field this + 0xc0, and opens PageSetupDlgA through FUN_0041a43b for control 0x424. After PrintDlgA, caller branches to textual equation printing or gate-diagram printing.

Inferred responsibility: Print-cancel modeless dialog procedure trampoline for PRNCNCLDLG; it forwards dialog messages to the print controller and lets the user cancel an active print job.
Evidence: FUN_0041ac36 and FUN_0041b260 create PRNCNCLDLG with CreateDialogParamA(..., FUN_0040af1b, 0) during printing, disable the main window, store the dialog handle at print-controller offset +4, set cancel flag +0xb4 to 0, then call SetAbortProc(..., FUN_0040af38). FUN_0040af1b calls FUN_0041c6a8(&DAT_00453ce8, hwnd, msg). FUN_0041c6a8 sets title "Logic Friday" on init and on command sets cancel flag +0xb4 to 1, re-enables owner, destroys the dialog, and clears the handle.

Inferred responsibility: Win32 printer abort procedure callback for Logic Friday print jobs; pumps the UI message queue during printing and tells GDI whether printing should continue, honoring the print-cancel dialog state.
Evidence: Body is FUN_0041c712(0x453ce8), using global print/controller object DAT_00453ce8. Both print paths call SetAbortProc(HDC, FUN_0040af38) after creating PRNCNCLDLG and before StartDocA. FUN_0041c712 loops PeekMessageA, routes messages through IsDialogMessageA for the cancel dialog, calls TranslateMessage/DispatchMessageA, and returns cancelFlag == 0. The cancel dialog proc FUN_0040af1b -> FUN_0041c6a8 sets that flag to 1.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure wrapper for the NAMESORDERDLG choice dialog used when two functions have the same input variable names but in a different order; lets the caller distinguish cancel, operate by matching variable names, or use raw truth-table input ordering.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_00416425. FUN_00416425 returns TRUE on WM_INITDIALOG, and on WM_COMMAND ends the dialog with result 2, 0x40b, or 0x40c. Call sites pass it to DialogBoxParamA(..., "NAMESORDERDLG", ...) after FUN_00416134 returns 1, meaning all input names exist in both functions but at different indexes. Dialog result 0x40b makes caller pass 1 to FUN_004161ea to remap the second function truth-table rows by matching names; result 0x40c keeps direct truth-table ordering; cancel aborts.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure wrapper for the compare-functions result dialog COMPRESDLG. It delegates to FUN_004164b1, which displays whether two selected one-output functions are equal, subsets, complements, or unequal, plus counts/labels for shared and function-only truth-table cases; command 0x40e closes with nonzero result so the caller can create/export the comparison result function.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_004164b1(&DAT_00452c50, hwnd, msg, command). Caller FUN_00415dd9 is reached from menu command 0xbb after exactly two functions are selected; it validates same input count and one output, reconciles input ordering if needed, compares truth-table values, sets result fields, then opens DialogBoxParamA(..., "COMPRESDLG", ..., FUN_0040af6c, 0). Delegate writes strings such as "%s equals %s.", "%s is a subset of %s.", "%s is the complement of %s.", "%s is not equal to %s.", "%s and %s:", and "%s only:" to controls 0x410..0x417.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure wrapper for the NEWFCNNAME dialog used during function operations to collect the name for a newly created result function. It delegates all behavior to FUN_00416ff2.
Evidence: Only call site found is DialogBoxParamA(..., "NEWFCNNAME", ..., FUN_0040af89, local_20) inside FUN_00416a26 after two selected functions are validated for a function operation. FUN_00416ff2 handles WM_INITDIALOG by storing lParam in DAT_0046c510, limiting edit control 0x418 to 8 chars, initializing the buffer and setting edit text. On OK it reads GetDlgItemTextA(hwnd, 0x418, DAT_0046c510, 9) and EndDialog(..., 1); on cancel it EndDialog(..., 2). Caller copies local_20 into the new function record name field.

Inferred responsibility: Win32 dialog procedure wrapper for LOOKUPFCNDLG, the Generate C Lookup Function options dialog. It delegates to FUN_004170c1, which lets the user choose lookup-table packing width and generated C function call style.
Evidence: Body delegates to FUN_004170c1(&DAT_00452c50, hwnd, msg, wParam, lParam). Caller opens DialogBoxParamA(..., "LOOKUPFCNDLG", ..., FUN_0040afa6, 0x4519a4) from Generate C Lookup Function flow; before dialog _DAT_004519b0 is set to selected function input count, and after OK the option block DAT_004519a4 is passed to FUN_004133f5, which writes C lookup functions. FUN_004170c1 initializes radio groups 0x419..0x41c and 0x420..0x423, validates/customizes unsigned-int length 8..64, warns for non-byte-aligned custom lengths, updates the option block, and opens Creating_lookup.htm help.

Inferred responsibility: `DiagInWindow` window procedure.
Evidence: Registered under `"DiagInWindow"` by `FUN_0040145f`; used for diagram-entry interaction.

Inferred responsibility: `DiagOutWindow` window procedure.
Evidence: Registered under `"DiagOutWindow"` by `FUN_0040145f`; receives application-defined command messages from the main dispatcher.

Inferred responsibility: Dialog procedure for the EQNMODVAR modal notice shown after modifying an equation when the input variable order may have changed; it only closes on OK/Cancel and opens help for variable ordering syntax.
Evidence: Used only as DialogBoxParamA(..., "EQNMODVAR", ..., FUN_0040b055, 0). Body returns TRUE on WM_INITDIALOG, closes with EndDialog(..., 1) for OK and EndDialog(..., 2) for Cancel, and on control 0x490 opens %s\\lf.chm::/Syntax.htm#VarOrder via the HTML Help wrapper. Caller shows it when FUN_0040e7da(oldFunction, newFunction) indicates same variables in a different order and the new equation text does not contain INORDER.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the RichEdit logic-equation entry control. It changes the cursor to wait during busy operations and intercepts equation-entry keyboard shortcuts: Enter submits, Ctrl+Enter inserts newline, Escape cancels, and Ctrl+V invokes custom paste; otherwise it forwards to the original RichEdit proc.
Evidence: FUN_00439623 creates DAT_00452ab0 as a RichEdit-backed text control, then DAT_00452ab0 is subclassed with SetWindowLongA(..., 0x40b12d), saving old proc in DAT_00452918. FUN_0040b12d handles WM_SETCURSOR with wait cursor when busy flags are set. WM_KEYDOWN is handled only when DAT_00452e98 is active during equation entry. Enter posts command 0x8000 to parse/set equation, Escape sends command 0x150 cancel path, Ctrl+V posts command 0xae custom paste path, and unhandled messages call CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452918, ...).

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the main function-list ListView; while long-running operations are active it forces the wait cursor on WM_SETCURSOR, otherwise it forwards messages to the original ListView procedure.
Evidence: During main-window initialization, FUN_0040e929 creates DAT_00452ac0 as a SysListView32 with columns such as Function, Inputs, Outputs, False, and Gates; SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452ac0, -4, 0x40b2da) installs this function and stores old proc in DAT_00452aa8. Function handles only WM_SETCURSOR: if busy flags DAT_00452e7c, DAT_00452eb0, DAT_00452eb4, DAT_00452ed0, or DAT_00452ed4 are set, it loads IDC_WAIT and returns 1; otherwise it calls CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452aa8, ...).

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the truth-table output SysListView32; while long-running operations are active it forces the wait cursor on WM_SETCURSOR, otherwise forwards messages to the original list-view window procedure.
Evidence: Startup creates two SysListView32 controls via FUN_00417acd; the first handle is DAT_00452ab4, then subclassed with SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452ab4, GWL_WNDPROC, 0x40b34e), storing old proc in DAT_00452aa4. FUN_0040b34e handles WM_SETCURSOR when busy flags are set, calls LoadCursorA(NULL, 0x7f02) / SetCursor, returns 1, and otherwise calls CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452aa4, ...). Main-window notification handling identifies DAT_00452ab4 as truth-table output by loading context menu MENU_TTOUT when focused.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure for the truth-table entry ListView; intercepts Escape and routes it to the main window as the truth-table cancel command, otherwise delegates to the original ListView window procedure.
Evidence: Startup FUN_00417acd creates two SysListView32 controls for truth-table UI, then SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452ab8, -4, 0x40b3c2) subclasses the second one and stores old proc in DAT_00452aa0. Body checks WM_KEYDOWN and VK_ESCAPE, sends WM_COMMAND 0xe6 to main window DAT_00452aac, and returns 1; all other messages call CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452aa0, ...). Main command handling treats 0xe6 as the cancel truth-table entry path; resources include truth-table menu items Cancel Esc and Submit Enter.

Inferred responsibility: Subclass window procedure that forces the wait cursor over a specific child/control window while long-running Logic Friday operations are active; otherwise forwards messages to the original window procedure.
Evidence: Installed with DAT_00452a9c = SetWindowLongA(DAT_00452a28, GWL_WNDPROC, 0x40b41f). Body handles only WM_SETCURSOR when busy flags DAT_00452e7c, DAT_00452eb0, DAT_00452eb4, DAT_00452ed0, or DAT_00452ed4 are set, loads IDC_WAIT, calls SetCursor, and returns 1. Otherwise delegates with CallWindowProcA(DAT_00452a9c, ...). Same wait-cursor subclass pattern appears in FUN_0040b12d, FUN_0040b2da, and FUN_0040b34e.
